text_contains: pattern: pop reg; pop reg; ret
false_positive_boundaries: Only distinct ordered pairs from the supported argument-register subset are promoted.
Duplicate or unsupported leading pops retain the strongest supported suffix rather than a multi-pop semantic claim.
ordered_multi_pop.score_policy: Ordered two-pop argument control is scored 95 after ordered controls, stack cost, clobbers, and effects validate.

false_positive_boundaries: Only exact 64-bit register-direct moves between distinct non-RSP registers are promoted.
Memory forms are reconciled through the memory family; self, RSP, and 32-bit forms remain fallbacks.
register_transfer.score_policy: Register transfers remain unscored until source-value controllability is modeled.

text_contains: side effects: stack_read, stack_adjust, flags_write, control_transfer
false_positive_boundaries: Only positive nonzero eight-byte-aligned imm8 additions to RSP are promoted.
Zero, negative, unaligned, wrong-register, subtraction, and wider-immediate forms remain fallbacks.
stack_adjust.score_policy: Positive aligned stack adjustments are scored 35 after exact immediate, flags, and stack facts validate.

false_positive_boundaries: Only exact qword base-plus-zero forms without SIB, displacement, RIP-relative addressing, or RSP participation are promoted.
Address, value, and memory-content controllability are not inferred.
memory.score_policy: Memory families remain unscored until address/value control and dereference risk are represented.

false_positive_boundaries: Exact effects describe the retained exact suffix and do not prove the full backward window decodes from its first byte.
Eight exact single-pop forms preserve unknown semantics while retaining deterministic stack and register effects.
exact_pattern_effects.score_policy: The catalog preserves existing scores plus reviewed multi-pop and stack-adjust scores; transfer and memory families remain null.
